data.title: SoFi Will Report Q1 Earnings on April 29. A Look at Top Shareholders Ahead of It
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI), a digital financial services and fintech company, is set to report its first-quarter 2026 earnings on Wednesday, April 29. The stock has declined about 30% so far in 2026. Ahead of the report, investors remain cautious due to the stock's high valuation and weaker mortgage market, despite improving profits. Meanwhile, Wall Street expects SoFi to report earnings of $0.12 per share on revenues of $1.05 billion. With the earnings report in focus, it is worth exploring who owns SOFI stock.

data.title: DeepSeek Cuts Prices on Latest AI Model as China Price War Erupts
data.description:
DeepSeek, a Chinese artificial intelligence (AI) startup, has slashed the prices of all its models, including its new model V4-Pro. The private company is cutting down costs to attract more developers and enterprise users. The reduced rates also come as AI competition surges among companies in China, with many opting for affordability to appeal more to users. 

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

DeepSeek Slashes Cost to Boost Adoption

DeepSeek announced that it has cut the price of input cache hits across its entire API suite to one-tenth of its previous price, with the change taking effect immediately. The move will help lower computing costs and attract more developers and enterprise users to its AI models. 

At the same time, the company said its recently launched V4-Pro model will remain available at a 75% discount until May 5. The extension is part of its wider push to grow adoption quickly and expand its market share. 

More broadly, many AI companies are now shifting from early testing to real-world adoption of large language models (LLMs). Because of this, reducing inference and usage costs has become a key factor in staying competitive in the AI sector. 

Price War Intensifies Across the Global AI Market

DeepSeek's price cuts are expected to push industry rivals to do the same, especially in China, where companies are rapidly building alternatives to Western technologies. This is because most firms are trying to attract more clients and developers by offering cheaper, more efficient AI tools. 

As such, price reductions have now become a key competitive strategy in the sector, alongside performance and scalability. This trend is also being shaped by geopolitical factors, including U.S. tech restrictions, which have accelerated the growth of local AI ecosystems in China. 

Which AI Stocks Can I Buy Now? 

Wall Street analysts rate Nvidia (NVDA), Alphabet Class A (GOOGL), and Meta Platforms (META) as Strong Buy, based on TipRanks consensus data. Among these stocks, NVDA has the highest upside potential of 31.3%, with an average price target of $273.5. For more information on the performances, ratings, and price targets of each of these stocks, visit TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: DPZ Earnings: Domino's Pizza Stock Stumbles on Q1 Misses
data.description:
Domino's Pizza (DPZ) stock took a beating on Monday following the release of the food chain's Q1 2026 earnings report. The company reported diluted earnings per share of $4.13, compared to Wall Street's estimate of $4.27. Diluted EPS for the quarter was also down 4.6% year-over-year from $4.33.

Domino's Pizza also reported revenue of $1.15 billion, which was another miss compared to analysts' estimate of $1.16 billion. Even so, the company's revenue did increase 3.5% year-over-year from $1.11 billion. Domino's Pizza attributed its revenue growth to "higher supply chain revenues and higher global franchise royalties and advertising revenues."
data.author: William White

data.title: Top Analyst Downgrades AMD Stock Despite Strong Near-Term Outlook — Here's Why
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), the chipmaker, is facing a more cautious view from top Northland analyst Gus Richard despite strong near-term momentum. The 5-star analyst downgraded the stock to Market Perform from Outperform and set a $260 price target, citing concerns about growth beyond 2026.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Trade NVDA with leverage

Notably, AMD stock closed 14% higher on Friday and gained 22% over the past five days.

Top Analyst Warns of Slower AI Spending Ahead

Richard expects AMD to deliver solid results in the near term and likely beat estimates in its upcoming report, scheduled for May 5. However, he believes the outlook may not be as strong as recent results from Intel (INTC), which has been gaining ground.

Looking ahead, the analyst sees growing risks for AMD's business. He expects AI infrastructure spending to slow in 2027 as large tech firms rein in capital spending and focus more on returns. At the same time, companies like OpenAI and Anthropic are shifting toward tighter pricing models, which could limit demand growth and weigh on AMD.

Richard also pointed to rising competition. Nvidia (NVDA) remains a dominant player and continues to secure a large share of chip supply from TSMC (TSM), which could limit AMD's growth. Meanwhile, Intel is improving its position, which may pressure AMD's pricing and margins.

While AMD's fundamentals remain strong, Richard believes expectations for 2027 are too high. He noted that ongoing R&D spending and competitive pressures could limit margin expansion, even as the company benefits from AI demand in the near term.

Is AMD Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for AMD is Strong Buy, based on 19 Buy and eight Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average AMD stock price target of $295.04, representing a potential 15.17% downside for the shares.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: AstraZeneca Stock ($AZN) Flattens Despite Green Light for Injector Pen to Stop "Killer" Lupus
data.description:
Shares in pharmaceuticals giant AstraZeneca (AZN) failed to get better today in pre-market trading despite its new self-treatment for lupus – a major killer of young women – being approved in the U.S.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Lupus Autoinjector Wins Approval

AstraZeneca said its new once-weekly autoinjector pen called Saphnelo for the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) in adults had been given the green light by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. It is already approved in the EU and Japan, and is under regulatory review in several other countries around the world.

The FDA approval was based on results from AZN's Phase III TULIP-SC trial, which revealed that the use of the pen had led to a "statistically significant and clinically meaningful reduction in disease activity."

Susan Manzi, MD, MPH, chair of the Allegheny Health Network (AHN) Medicine Institute, director of the Lupus Center of Excellence at the AHN Autoimmunity Institute and principal investigator of the TULIP-SC trial, said: "The approval makes this important medicine more convenient and accessible for many more patients. With its proven ability to significantly reduce disease activity and the risk of organ damage, it has been a much-needed innovation in lupus, which is a serious and often debilitating autoimmune condition impacting millions worldwide."

Leading Killer of Young Women

Ruud Dobber, Executive Vice President, BioPharmaceuticals Business Unit, AstraZeneca, added: "Since its launch, Saphnelo IV infusion has helped tens of thousands of people with systemic lupus erythematosus achieve lower disease activity with fewer steroids and has been shown to help many achieve remission."

SLE, is an autoimmune diseases in which the immune system attacks healthy tissue in the body. Over 3.4 million people globally are affected by SLE and is amongst the leading causes of death in young women in the US. It is more common amongst Asian, Black or Hispanic populations.

The approval comes just ahead of AZN's Q1 earnings report on April 29.  It is expected to report post quarterly earnings of $2.57 per share, which will indicate a year-over-year increase of 3.6%. Revenues are expected to be $14.98 billion, up 10.2% from the year-ago quarter.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Stock Market News, 4/27/26 – U.S Stock Futures Edge Lower as Oil Climbs on Stalled U.S.-Iran Talks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down early Monday as oil prices moved higher. Oil climbed after Iran peace talks stalled, while fresh tensions near the Strait of Hormuz added to concerns about possible supply disruptions. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 0.13%, 0.09%, and 0.17%, respectively, at 4:30 a.m. EDT on April 27.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

The dip in futures follows news that President Donald Trump canceled a planned trip by U.S. envoys for Iran ceasefire talks, saying negotiations could instead take place over the phone. Meanwhile, Iranian officials said no meetings with the U.S. are currently scheduled.

At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) increased by 2.88% to $108.36 per barrel as tensions rose after Iran boarded two container ships. Meanwhile, WTI crude (CM:CL) gained 2.61% to $96.86.

Beyond geopolitics, Wall Street is heading into a busy week. Five of the "Magnificent Seven" tech giants are set to report earnings, putting the spotlight on whether their results can support current valuations. Investors will be closely watching updates from Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), Meta Platforms (META), and Apple (AAPL).

In addition to earnings, the Federal Reserve will meet this Wednesday to discuss interest rates. This is expected to be Jerome Powell's last meeting as Chair before Kevin Warsh takes over in May.

Last week, the Dow Jones fell about 0.44%, ending its three-week winning streak, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq both hit new record highs. The S&P 500 advanced 0.55% for the week, while the Nasdaq-100 jumped 2.37%, driven by a massive rally in semiconductor stocks.  

Elsewhere, European markets inched higher on Monday as investors monitored developments in the Middle East.

Asia-Pacific Markets Mostly Higher

Asia-Pacific markets were mostly higher on Monday, as investors largely looked past tensions between the U.S. and Iran that pushed oil prices up.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index slipped 0.20%, while Japan's Topix rose 0.50% and the Nikkei 225 gained 1.38%. In China, the Shanghai Composite added 0.16%, and the Shenzhen Component climbed 0.37%.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
